'''Tharil Tavira''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] who was appointed by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to oversee [[Eiattu 6]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Although married, he began an affair with a young native [[Gender/Legends|girl]] named [[Leonia Tavira|Leonia]], whom he married when his [[Tharil Tavira's first wife|wife]] [[Death/Legends|died]] suddenly and unexpectedly. Shortly afterward, he suffered a massive stroke that paralyzed him.

Due to a rare [[Allergy/Legends|allergy]] to [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]], it was impossible to treat him. Facing a very long and painful recovery process with no guarantee of success, Tharil eventually killed himself. He was succeeded as Governor of Eiattu by Leonia, who was suspected by some of having engineered both his first wife's death and his stroke.

==Behind the scenes==
It is sometimes claimed that Grand Moff Tavira controlled not just [[Eiattu 6]] but all of [[Ado sector/Legends|Ado sector]]; however, there seems to be no [[canon]] evidence for this, and his [[Oversector/Legends|priority sector]] may have been limited to the one [[Planet/Legends|planet]], or perhaps simply its [[Star system/Legends|star system]].

There is also some uncertainty about Tharil Tavira's rank: he is identified as a [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] when first referred to in the story, and the badge Leonia is normally shown wearing is appropriate for this rank, but she herself is only ever called simply a [[Moff/Legends|Moff]].

Tavira is referred to as "Thavil Tavira" in ''[[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]''.
